mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 320 signals persistent inflation, which erodes the real return of bonds, causing investors to demand higher nominal yields and driving the 10-year yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day yield changes are negative in more than 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL is above 320, or rank IC remains below 0.03 over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 4.5%, it signals economic weakness, triggering a flight-to-quality into long-term Treasuries, which pushes the 10-year yield downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Yield rises in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the average 20-day return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) rise, but retail sales grow faster relative to industrial production, the demand-supply imbalance creates inflationary pressure, lifting inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The relative growth differential between RSAFS and INDPRO shows no predictive power (rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (UNRATE at or below 4.0%), an increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) signals further overheating, intensifying wage inflation expectations and driving the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE ≤ 4.0% and PAYEMS is in its top quintile, yields do not rise in more than 50% of cases over the next 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening that is expected to slow the economy and reduce long-term growth and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year yield to decline over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the 5-day yield return is positive in more than 40% of cases or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 320, it indicates entrenched inflation, which forces the Federal Reserve to maintain a tight policy stance; the resulting higher term premium pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day yield change is negative in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the hit rate is below 55% over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust aggregate demand and labor market tightness, which together increase inflation expectations and push the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of HOUST above 1300 and UNRATE below 4.5% predicts yield direction correctly less than 55% of the time over the next 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: During an easing cycle defined by FEDFUNDS at or below 3.0%, a rise in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates that monetary easing is stimulating demand and may reduce the need for further cuts, causing yields to rise as expectations of continued accommodation diminish over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <= 3.0% and RSAFS rises month-over-month, the 5-day yield change is negative in more than 60% of instances.
